thought: Most engineers think more data always leads to better AI models, but I think that's a gross oversimplification. Doesn't the ease of obtaining data just mask our inability to design meaningful experiments, what are we really optimizing for?
Most think more layers means better neural networks, but added complexity often hinders interpretability, what if simplicity is the real key to AI breakthroughs?

Buffer overflows are being exploited. They occur when more data is written to a buffer than it is designed to hold, causing extra data to spill over into adjacent areas of memory, potentially allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code. This can lead to code execution and privilege escalation. CVE-2014-0160 affects OpenSSL 1.0.1
